purpose: LAG/PortChannel management via Linux teamd with LACP negotiation. Runs one teamd instance per configured PortChannel, manages LACP PDU exchange with remote peers, and keeps APPL_DB LAG_TABLE and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE synchronized so orchagent can program SAI LAG objects. Acts as the bridge between Linux bonding semantics and SONiC's SAI-based forwarding plane.

daemons.name: teammgrd
daemons.role: Config-to-teamd bridge. Watches CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL_TABLE and PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER_TABLE for create/delete/modify events. Translates them into teamd JSON configuration files and spawns or terminates per-PortChannel teamd instances via D-Bus. Also handles admin-state changes by signaling teamd to enable/disable LACP PDU sending.

writes: Linux: /etc/teamd/<PortChannel>.conf, teamd process lifecycle via D-Bus

daemons.name: teamsyncd
daemons.role: Teamd-to-APPL_DB bridge. Subscribes to teamd netlink events for LAG member state transitions (link up/down within a PortChannel, LACP state changes). Translates these events into APPL_DB LAG_TABLE and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE writes so orchagent LagOrch can update SAI LAG membership and PortChannel operational state in hardware.

daemons.name: teamd (per-PortChannel instances)
daemons.role: One teamd process per configured PortChannel. Runs the LACP state machine for each member port: sends and receives LACP PDUs, tracks partner system ID/key/port priority, transitions member ports between LACP_COLLECTING_DISTRIBUTING and LACP_DEFAULTED states. Each instance reads its config from /etc/teamd/<PortChannel>.conf written by teammgrd. Crashes of individual instances affect only the associated PortChannel.

writes: Linux kernel: team netdevice member state via teamd netlink API
Linux: teamd D-Bus state (consumed by teamsyncd)

key_data_flows.path: config add portchannel -> CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL_TABLE -> teammgrd -> /etc/teamd/PortChannelN.conf + teamd process spawn -> teamd LACP negotiation -> LACP_COLLECTING_DISTRIBUTING -> teamsyncd (netlink) -> APPL_DB:LAG_TABLE + LAG_MEMBER_TABLE -> orchagent:LagOrch -> ASIC_DB:ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG -> syncd -> SAI -> ASIC
key_data_flows.notes: LACP negotiation must complete before teamsyncd writes the member as active. If partner does not respond, member stays in LACP_DEFAULTED and is not added to the hardware LAG.

key_data_flows.path: Physical link down -> teamd detects via netlink RTM_NEWLINK -> teamd marks member LACP state inactive -> teamsyncd receives teamd netlink event -> APPL_DB:LAG_MEMBER_TABLE updated (status=down) -> orchagent:LagOrch removes member from SAI LAG -> ASIC stops hashing traffic to that member
key_data_flows.notes: Latency from physical link-down to ASIC update is typically under 1 second. teamsyncd -> orchagent -> syncd pipeline adds ~200ms on loaded systems.

key_data_flows.flow: LACP PDU timeout causing member removal
key_data_flows.path: Partner stops sending PDUs -> lacp-actor-timeout expires (3s short / 90s long) -> teamd transitions member to LACP_EXPIRED then LACP_DEFAULTED -> teamsyncd writes member status=down to APPL_DB -> orchagent removes member from SAI LAG -> traffic stops using that member
key_data_flows.notes: Short timers (fast LACP) use 3-second timeout. Long timers use 90 seconds. Mismatched timer modes between actor and partner cause repeated expiry/re-establishment cycles.

key_data_flows.path: CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL_INTF_TABLE -> intfmgrd (in swss) -> APPL_DB:INTF_TABLE -> orchagent:IntfsOrch -> ASIC_DB -> syncd -> SAI router interface
key_data_flows.notes: IP address on a PortChannel is handled entirely by swss intfmgrd, not teamd. teamd only manages LAG membership. If PortChannel has members and is up but has no L3 reachability, check swss intfmgrd and orchagent, not teamd.

warm_restart.mechanism: teammgrd and teamsyncd support warm restart. On warm restart, teamd instances are NOT restarted -- they continue running and maintaining LACP sessions with peers. teammgrd reconciles CONFIG_DB state against running teamd instances. teamsyncd re-subscribes to teamd netlink events without writing a full LAG_TABLE flush to APPL_DB, preventing unnecessary LagOrch churn in orchagent.
warm_restart.caveat: Warm restart is only effective if teamd instances remain running. If any teamd instance crashed before the warm restart, it will be respawned and LACP re-negotiated from scratch for that PortChannel, causing brief traffic loss on affected LAG members.

restart_impact: Cold restart terminates all per-PortChannel teamd instances, causing all PortChannels to immediately lose their members from APPL_DB and ASIC_DB. orchagent removes SAI LAG members, traffic hashing stops. LACP sessions with all peers are torn down (partner detects PDU loss). On container restart, teamd instances respawn, LACP re-negotiates (1-3 seconds for short timers, up to 90 seconds for long timers), then teamsyncd repopulates APPL_DB. With warm restart enabled, teamd instances survive and LACP sessions are maintained -- APPL_DB is reconciled without hardware disruption.

restart_commands.restart_single_portchannel: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannelN state dump (inspect first); sudo systemctl restart teamd@PortChannelN (if using systemd service per-LAG)
restart_commands.warm_restart: sudo warm-reboot (system-level) or config warm_restart enable teamd && docker restart teamd

health_check_commands: docker exec teamd ps aux | grep teamd
state_verification_commands: show interfaces portchannel (PortChannel operational state and members)
show lacp neighbor (partner system ID, key, port state)
show lacp internal (actor state, port priority, key)
sonic-db-cli ASIC_DB keys 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG*' (SAI LAG objects in ASIC_DB)
docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel1 state dump (raw teamd LACP state per PortChannel)
docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel1 config dump (running teamd config for PortChannel)

common_issues.issue: teamd instance crash -- single PortChannel down
symptoms: One PortChannel goes operationally down while others remain up
show interfaces portchannel shows specific PortChannel as 'D' with no active members
docker exec teamd ps aux shows no teamd process for the affected PortChannel
APPL_DB LAG_TABLE entry for affected PortChannel disappears or shows status=down
orchagent removes SAI LAG members -- traffic hashing to that LAG stops
Member physical interfaces remain individually up (not a physical link issue)
causes: teamd process segfault due to malformed LACP PDU from partner (uncommon but seen with certain vendor NICs)
Corrupt /etc/teamd/<PortChannel>.conf written by teammgrd (e.g. during partial config update)
OOM kill of individual teamd instance under memory pressure
teammgrd wrote invalid runner or link_watch config (e.g. unsupported LACP mode combination)
diagnosis: docker exec teamd ps aux | grep teamd -- identify which PortChannel's teamd is missing
ls -lt /var/core/*.core -- check for recent teamd core files
tail -100 /var/log/teamd/teamd_PortChannel1.log -- look for 'SIGSEGV', 'assert', 'failed'
cat /etc/teamd/PortChannel1.conf -- verify config JSON is well-formed
dmesg | grep -i 'oom\|killed\|teamd' -- check for OOM kill
redis-cli -n 0 hgetall 'LAG_TABLE:PortChannel1' -- confirm APPL_DB entry is absent
resolution: Respawn crashed teamd instance: docker exec teamd supervisorctl restart teamd@PortChannel1 (or restart full teamd container if supervisorctl shows FATAL)
If config corrupt: config portchannel del PortChannel1 && config portchannel add PortChannel1 -- forces teammgrd to regenerate conf file
For OOM: show processes memory | grep teamd; reduce number of PortChannels or add swap
If recurring crash: capture core file for vendor analysis; temporarily convert to static LAG (no LACP) as workaround: config portchannel add PortChannel1 --static

common_issues.issue: LACP negotiation failure -- PDUs sent but partner not responding
symptoms: show lacp neighbor shows empty or stale partner information for affected PortChannel
show lacp internal shows actor in LACP_DEFAULTED or LACP_EXPIRED state
show interfaces portchannel shows PortChannel with 0 active members despite physical links up
teamd log shows repeated 'LACP PDU timeout' or 'partner info expired' messages
Traffic not flowing over PortChannel despite links being physically up
causes: LACP timer mismatch: actor configured for short (fast) timers, partner using long (slow) timers or vice versa
System priority or port priority mismatch causing port selection algorithm to exclude ports on one side
Partner switch has LACP disabled or interface configured as access (not trunk with LACP)
LACP PDUs being dropped by intermediate device (e.g. LACP not passing through a media converter or optical tap)
Mismatched LACP key (admin_key) between actor and partner -- ports with different keys cannot form a LAG together
Partner in passive mode while actor also in passive mode -- neither side initiates
diagnosis: show lacp internal -- check actor_key, system_priority, port_priority, LACP mode (active/passive)
show lacp neighbor -- if empty, partner PDUs not arriving; if present but stale, check timestamp
docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel1 state dump -- raw LACP state machine details
tcpdump -i Ethernet0 ether proto 0x8809 -- capture LACP PDUs on member interface; verify both directions
Compare actor lacp-rate with partner config: redis-cli -n 4 hgetall 'PORTCHANNEL|PortChannel1' -- check fast_rate field
show lacp neighbor -- compare partner system_id, key, port_num with expected values
resolution: Timer mismatch: config portchannel member del PortChannel1 Ethernet0; config portchannel member add PortChannel1 Ethernet0 -- re-negotiate; ensure both sides use same lacp-rate (fast or slow)
To set fast LACP: redis-cli -n 4 hset 'PORTCHANNEL|PortChannel1' fast_rate true (then restart teamd for PortChannel)
Both passive: set one side to active mode -- SONiC default is active; verify partner is not also passive
Key mismatch: ensure all member ports on both sides belong to same logical LAG group; admin_key is auto-derived from LAG ID in SONiC
If PDUs not arriving: check intermediate devices pass Ethernet multicast 01:80:c2:00:00:02 (LACP MAC)

common_issues.issue: teamsyncd not updating APPL_DB -- LAG member state changes not reaching orchagent
symptoms: Physical member links change state but show interfaces portchannel does not update
docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel1 state dump shows correct member state but APPL_DB is stale
redis-cli -n 0 hgetall 'LAG_MEMBER_TABLE:PortChannel1:Ethernet0' shows outdated status
orchagent not receiving LAG member updates -- ASIC continues hashing to down member
teamsyncd process running (supervisorctl shows RUNNING) but not processing events
causes: teamsyncd lost its netlink subscription to teamd generic netlink family (TEAMD_GENL_NAME) -- requires restart
Redis connection from teamsyncd to APPL_DB lost or blocked (database container issue)
teamsyncd stuck in a processing loop on a malformed netlink message from a teamd instance
teamd instance for the affected PortChannel restarted without teamsyncd re-subscribing to its events
diagnosis: docker exec teamd supervisorctl status teamsyncd -- verify process is RUNNING not FATAL
tail -50 /var/log/teamd/teamsyncd.log -- look for 'netlink error', 'redis connection', 'failed to receive'
docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel1 state dump -- compare member states vs APPL_DB
redis-cli -n 0 hgetall 'LAG_MEMBER_TABLE:PortChannel1:Ethernet0' -- check current APPL_DB value
redis-cli -n 0 hgetall 'LAG_TABLE:PortChannel1' -- verify oper_status field
strace -p <teamsyncd_pid> -e trace=network -- check if netlink socket is receiving events (advanced)
resolution: Restart teamsyncd: docker exec teamd supervisorctl restart teamsyncd
If teamsyncd restart does not resolve: restart full teamd container (sudo systemctl restart teamd) -- teamsyncd will re-subscribe to all per-PortChannel teamd instances
Verify database container health before restarting: redis-cli -n 0 ping -- should return PONG
After restart: verify teamsyncd repopulates APPL_DB: redis-cli -n 0 keys 'LAG_MEMBER_TABLE*'

common_issues.issue: PortChannel flapping -- repeated partner timeout and re-establishment
symptoms: show interfaces portchannel shows PortChannel cycling between U (up) and D (down) periodically
teamd log shows repeated 'LACP timeout' followed by 'LACP established' messages for same PortChannel
lacp-actor-timeout expiry events appearing in /var/log/teamd/teamd_PortChannel1.log every few seconds or minutes
Intermittent traffic loss on affected PortChannel correlating with flap events
show lacp neighbor shows partner info present then clearing then reappearing
Syslog shows repeated LAG_TABLE:PortChannel1 status transitions
causes: Marginal physical link: member port receiving LACP PDUs intermittently due to dirty fiber/connector or flapping SFP -- PDU gaps trigger timeout
Partner switch CPU overloaded: LACP PDU generation delayed beyond lacp-actor-timeout (3s for fast, 90s for slow) causing repeated expiry
Fast LACP timer mismatch under load: one side generating PDUs every 1s but partner's timer starts at 3x = 3s -- marginal link delay pushes past 3s
MTU mismatch on member interface causing LACP PDUs (64-byte frames) to pass but larger frames to drop -- PDUs still arrive but the misconfiguration indicates deeper connectivity issue
Unidirectional link: LACP PDUs sent but not received in one direction -- teamd sees outgoing PDUs acknowledged only when partner sends its own PDUs
diagnosis: show interfaces counters Ethernet0 -- check for increasing input/output errors or CRC errors on member interfaces
grep -c 'timeout\|expired\|defaulted' /var/log/teamd/teamd_PortChannel1.log -- count flap events
show lacp internal -- check lacp-rate; if fast, consider switching to slow as workaround for marginal links
tcpdump -i Ethernet0 ether proto 0x8809 -c 20 -- verify PDUs arriving at regular 1s (fast) or 30s (slow) intervals
show interfaces counters errors -- look for CRC/symbol errors indicating physical layer issue
redis-cli -n 6 hgetall 'LAG_TABLE|PortChannel1' -- STATE_DB may show flap count or last-change timestamp
resolution: For marginal physical link: clean fiber connectors, replace SFP; check DOM diagnostics: show interfaces transceiver Ethernet0
Switch from fast to slow LACP timers to tolerate CPU/link jitter: redis-cli -n 4 hset 'PORTCHANNEL|PortChannel1' fast_rate false; restart teamd container
Partner CPU overload: reduce LACP-protected LAG count on partner or upgrade partner hardware; as workaround convert to static LAG: config portchannel add PortChannel1 --static
Unidirectional link: verify bidirectional light levels on both ends with show interfaces transceiver; check partner side for errors
Enable BFD on the PortChannel interface as a supplementary fast-failure detector (does not fix LACP but can trigger faster rerouting)

scale_considerations.max_portchannels: Platform-dependent. SONiC typically supports up to 32 PortChannels. Each PortChannel spawns one teamd process -- monitor total teamd process count with 'docker exec teamd ps aux | grep -c teamd'.
scale_considerations.max_members_per_portchannel: SAI and teamd support up to 8 member ports per PortChannel on most platforms. Exceeding this silently fails -- added member stays in CONFIG_DB but teamd will not activate it.
scale_considerations.lacp_pdu_rate: With fast LACP enabled, each member generates 1 PDU/second. On high-member-count deployments (e.g. 32 LAGs x 8 members = 256 PDUs/sec), verify partner switch CPU can sustain PDU processing.
scale_considerations.memory_per_instance: Each teamd instance uses approximately 5-10MB RSS. For 32 PortChannels, total teamd memory footprint is ~320MB. Monitor with 'show processes memory | grep teamd'.
